title: requested IP address 172.22.1.254 is not available in range set 172.22.1.1-172.22.1.254

comments.body: could you show the comand to reproduce it？

am just trying like this :
```
podman run --rm --network test-network --ip=172.22.1.254 httpd
```
and getting same message, I think those are not available because i assigned them before and even i removed those pods and containers but they are still assigned? how to reset the `cni-podman1` interfaces?

and from 
`"iptables -t nat -L"` i got this... though there all the 172.22.1.0 and 172.22.0.0 must be not there..

i think it is happened as follows:
Create the network
Create the pod
Create the containers in side the pod and the created network..
Now delete the network: it will ask you to add -f cause there are associated containers to it. 
Add -f and delete the network, this will delete the containers as will, but keeping the pod.
if you do this for the beginning whatever ip's taken in the first time it will stay reserved for not existing containers..
could you paste the full commands to repreduce it? It would be helpful
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mhmr is this still an issue, you did not respond to @zhangguanzhang, so I will close this issue, if we don't hear back in the next week.
